summary: Audit-only inspection of the current working tree autosave request path for /admin/blog/notion, focused on HTTP/API handlers and autosave-triggered public revalidation.

findings.summary: Each autosave mutation performs an extra browser-side session check before the blog PUT.
The protected blog update path is thin, but auth validation adds DB lookups and occasional writes around the actual blog update.
Autosave-triggered revalidation is throttled and does not block the Saved UI state.
When revalidation runs, nested auth/session fetches are more likely to dominate than the small revalidatePath/revalidateTag loops.
Cold-cache CSRF bootstrap can add another round trip, including for /revalidate-public even though antiforgery middleware does not target that route.

dominantSteps: Blog load + slug existence check + SaveChangesAsync
Throttled POST /revalidate-public and its nested session fetch
GET /api/auth/csrf on cold-cache or retry paths
recommendation: Profile and reduce repeated auth/session hops before optimizing excerpt/search-text processing or revalidate loops.
